Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The True Shape of Io's Steeple Mountain 86 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs 93 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Vector Meson Dominance 43 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Intel Assured Supply Chain Product Brief 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac 369 upvotes, 78 comments
  6. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1623 upvotes, 428 comments
  7. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 43 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. What Category Theory Teaches Us About DataFrames 78 upvotes, 18 comments
  9. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 561 upvotes, 193 comments
  10. NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns 167 upvotes, 50 comments
  11. LinkedIn is searching your browser extensions 1819 upvotes, 730 comments
  12. C89cc.sh – standalone C89/ELF64 compiler in pure portable shell 162 upvotes, 52 comments
  13. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 35 upvotes, 7 comments
  14. A Recipe for Steganogravy 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. Samsung Magician disk utility takes 18 steps and two reboots to uninstall 81 upvotes, 25 comments
  16. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 133 upvotes, 46 comments
  17. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 968 upvotes, 431 comments
  18. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 148 upvotes, 56 comments
  19. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 292 upvotes, 129 comments
  20. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 303 upvotes, 144 comments
  21. Tailscale's new macOS home 503 upvotes, 257 comments
  22. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  23. ESP32-S31: Dual-Core RISC-V SoC with Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, and Advanced HMI 117 upvotes, 62 comments
  24. HarfBuzz Slug Support with WebGL 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  25. Bun: cgroup-aware AvailableParallelism / HardwareConcurrency on Linux 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  26. Critics say EU risks ceding control of its tech laws under U.S. pressure 109 upvotes, 68 comments
  27. Cursor 3 475 upvotes, 352 comments
  28. Grok scored zero on ARC-AGI-3. Every 5-year-old did better 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Show HN: ctx – an Agentic Development Environment (ADE) 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  30. TDF ejects its core developers 70 upvotes, 54 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 3 April 2026. Source here.
